doc: Course: Introduction to Large Language Models. Description: In this module, you’ll explore the capabilities of large language models and their business applications. You’ll learn about the different types of IBM Granite models and the unique features that make them ideal for enterprise use. You’ll also discover how to craft effective prompts to guide these models and overcome common challenges in their use. Duration: 90 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Introduction to Large Language Models

doc: Course: Build Your First Chatbot Using IBM watsonx™. Description: In this course, you will learn the steps to create conversational chatbots that can understand and respond to natural language. You will also understand the fundamentals of Natural Language Processing (NLP) and learn how to apply them to real-world scenarios. Through a hands-on simulation using IBM watsonx, you’ll gain the skills needed to create a simple chatbot. Duration: 60 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Build Your First Chatbot Using IBM watsonx™

doc: Course: Prompt Engineering: Shaping Better AI Responses. Description: Prompt engineering is the key to getting AI to work smarter for you; by asking the right questions, you unlock more creative, accurate, and powerful responses. In this module, you’ll identify the applications of different types of prompts used in generative AI. You’ll explore different prompt engineering techniques and find out how to select a technique best suited to your specific task. Finally, you’ll explore some best practices for writing effective prompts. Duration: 4 hours. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Prompt Engineering: Shaping Better AI Responses

doc: Course: Turn Ideas Into Prototypes With Vibe Coding. Description: In this module, you’ll discover the benefits of using vibe coding for app development, explore how vibe coding transforms ideas into functional prototypes, and learn about the best practices in vibe coding. Duration: 60 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Turn Ideas Into Prototypes With Vibe Coding

doc: Course: Ethical Considerations for Using Generative AI. Description: In this module, you’ll explore the fascinating and complex world of ethics as they relate to generative AI. You’ll learn about the pillars of transparency, accountability, and fairness. You’ll discover ethical considerations and risks related to data inputs to AI systems and uncover the ethical implications of AI-generated output. Finally, you’ll be introduced to the IBM AI risk atlas. Duration: 60-90 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Ethical Considerations for Using Generative AI

doc: Course: Unleashing the Power of AI Agents. Description: In this module, you’ll explore AI agents, how they perform tasks, and the different types of AI agents that exist. You’ll learn how to identify key use cases where AI agents improve efficiency and categorize them based on how they make decisions. Finally, you’ll learn how AI agents work and where they are making an impact today. Duration: 60-90 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Unleashing the Power of AI Agents

doc: Course: The Rise of Multiagent Systems. Description: In this module, you learn about multiagent systems and how they differ from single-agent systems. You explore how agents interact and work together and discover the ways these systems solve complex problems across different industries. Through practical examples, you uncover the power of agents collaborating to create innovative solutions. Duration: 60-90 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: The Rise of Multiagent Systems

doc: Course: Vector Embeddings: AI’s Key to Meaning. Description: In this module, you learn about how when searching online, the words used in a query often don’t exactly match the words in the content being sought. That’s where vector embeddings come in. They help AI to capture meaning by recognizing patterns and relationships across different types of data, rather than focusing only on exact words. Duration: 60-90 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Vector Embeddings: AI’s Key to Meaning

doc: Course: Classifying Data Using IBM Granite. Description: In this module, you’ll explore the role of generative artificial intelligence in simplifying tasks involving classifying data. You’ll identify methods for selecting an IBM Granite model for classifying text-based data and examine various prompts to use within these models. Duration: 60 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Classifying Data Using IBM Granite

doc: Course: Introduction to Retrieval-Augmented Generation. Description: In this module, you’ll learn what RAG is and identify its key use cases. Next, you’ll explore the benefits of using RAG to enhance the knowledge base of an AI model. Finally, you’ll find out the key steps in the RAG workflow. Duration: 60-90 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Introduction to Retrieval-Augmented Generation

doc: Course: Explore Text to Speech Using IBM watson®. Description: This module introduces the fundamentals of text-to-speech (TTS) technology and the role of generative AI in developing these systems. Through a hands-on simulation using IBM watsonx, you’ll gain the skills needed to create and refine your own AI-powered voice synthesis model. Duration: 60 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Explore Text to Speech Using IBM watson®

doc: Course: IBM Granite Models for Software Development. Description: In this module, you’ll discover how generative artificial intelligence is rewriting the rules of code creation. You’ll learn about IBM Granite models and discover their different types and the benefits they bring to your coding journey. You’ll also learn how to use IBM Granite models to troubleshoot code, allowing you to identify and fix issues faster. You’ll discover the value of Granite models for software developers and be able to explain how to use them. Duration: 60 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: IBM Granite Models for Software Development

doc: Course: Summarizing Data Using IBM Granite. Description: In this module, you’ll explore the role of generative AI in text-based data summarization. Next, you’ll learn about various IBM Granite models for text summarization and how you can choose the right model for your needs. Finally, you’ll explore how to prompt IBM Granite models to summarize text. Duration: 60 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Summarizing Data Using IBM Granite

doc: Course: Use Generative AI for Software Development Using IBM watsonx™. Description: In this course, you’ll learn how AI can help you write new code, switch code between programming languages, and fix common coding issues. You’ll get a chance to practice using IBM watsonx in a simulation to generate code with the help of simple prompts. Duration: 60 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Use Generative AI for Software Development Using IBM watsonx™

doc: Course: AI in Healthcare: Improving Care and Efficiency. Description: In this module, you’ll discover the benefits of using AI in healthcare and review some use cases. You’ll also explore the key challenges of using AI in healthcare and find out how to address them effectively. Duration: 90 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: AI in Healthcare: Improving Care and Efficiency

doc: Course: AI in Legal: From Research to Results. Description: In this module, you’ll discover the benefits of using AI in the legal industry and some key use cases. You’ll also explore the potential concerns related to using AI for legal tasks and the ways to address the concerns. Duration: 90 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: AI in Legal: From Research to Results

doc: Course: The Power of Personalized Finance with AI. Description: Artificial intelligence is enabling financial institutions to tailor guidance, products, and support to individuals by analyzing behavior, anticipating needs, and adapting in real time. In this module, you’ll explore examples of personalized financial experiences, learn how different AI technologies make them possible, and discover which applications fit various financial settings. Duration: 60 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: The Power of Personalized Finance with AI

doc: Course: Transforming the Sports Fan Experience with AI. Description: Artificial intelligence is revolutionizing the sports fan experience by turning passive spectators into active participants. In this module, you’ll explore how AI is changing the way fans connect with sports. You’ll explore how evolving fan preferences are driving these changes and get a glimpse of future opportunities where AI could make the fan experience even more exciting and inclusive. Duration: 75 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Transforming the Sports Fan Experience with AI

doc: Course: AI’s New Superpower: Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation. Description: In this module, you’ll learn what multimodal RAG is and explore how it differs from text-only RAG. Next, you’ll discover the steps in a multimodal RAG system workflow and find out about some challenges associated with it. Finally, you’ll learn about some real-life applications of multimodal RAG. Duration: 90 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: AI’s New Superpower: Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation

doc: Course: Elevate Education with AI. Description: In this module, you’ll explore how educators use AI in classrooms. You’ll learn the four main forms of AI and how each one supports different educational needs. You’ll discover generative AI tools, including prompt patterns that can help you receive better output. You also examine challenges surrounding AI in education and how teachers, administrators, and schools can use AI responsibly. Duration: 75 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Elevate Education with AI

doc: Course: Risk Management and Fraud Detection using Generative AI. Description: Explore how generative AI is changing risk management and fraud detection. You’ll examine how you can train and use generative AI to supplement risk management and fraud detection work. You’ll investigate how you can apply generative AI capabilities to realistic risk management and fraud detection scenarios. Finally, you’ll learn about key concerns that can arise when you use generative AI in risk management and fraud detection. Duration: 90 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Risk Management and Fraud Detection using Generative AI

doc: Course: Getting Started with Artificial Intelligence. Description: This credential earner demonstrates a foundational understanding of Artificial Intelligence concepts and processes, including common applications of AI, and generative AI. The individual has worked with generative AI tools to refine and create prompts. Duration: 3 hours. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Getting Started with Artificial Intelligence

doc: Course: Getting Started with Generative AI. Description: This course introduces foundational concepts of generative AI, including how machines create text and images, the ethical considerations involved, and the use of tools like the IBM AI Risk Atlas. Learners will explore practical applications of large language models such as IBM Granite in areas like customer service and content creation. Duration: 3 hours. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Getting Started with Generative AI

doc: Course: Generative AI Essentials: Using LLMs to Work with Data. Description: Unlock the power of generative AI to simplify data analysis and transform your ability to extract insights from unstructured data. With this program, you’ll harness the unique elements of the data analysis tool, including its ability to handle multilingual data and perform sentiment analysis. Duration: 4 hours. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Generative AI Essentials: Using LLMs to Work with Data

doc: Course: AI Literacy. Description: Explore real-world use cases across industries and practice guiding an AI assistant to solve a hands-on business challenge. Whether you’re a student, job seeker, educator, or lifelong learner wanting to know more about AI, this course is for you, giving you the foundation to recognize, evaluate, and use AI responsibly. Duration: 4 hours. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: AI Literacy

doc: Course: Make Agentic AI Work for You. Description: Gain the skills to harness the power of AI agents, accelerate your understanding of RAG, and transform your approach to complex challenges with multiagent systems. With hands-on experience through simulation use cases, such as enhancing HR virtual assistants with RAG and automating procurement, you’ll be propelled to continue learning in this rapidly evolving field, driving meaningful impact and shaping the future of AI. Duration: 4 hours. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Make Agentic AI Work for You

doc: Course: Artificial Intelligence Fundamentals. Description: This credential earner demonstrates knowledge of artificial intelligence (AI) concepts, such as natural language processing, computer vision, machine learning, deep learning, chatbots, and neural networks; AI ethics; and the applications of AI. The individual has a conceptual understanding of how to run an AI model using IBM Watson Studio. The earner is aware of the job outlook in fields that use AI and is familiar with the skills required for success in various roles in the domain. Duration: 10+ hours. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Artificial Intelligence Fundamentals

doc: Course: Build Smarter AI with Embeddings. Description: In this module, you’ll explore the concept of vector embeddings and their role in AI. You’ll learn how to generate and optimize text embeddings using appropriate models and discover how image embeddings are created and fine-tuned for optimal performance. Duration: 3 hours 30 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Build Smarter AI with Embeddings

doc: Course: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Enhanced AI Outputs. Description: In this module, you’ll discover use cases for using RAG, explore the workflow a RAG system uses for content generation, and learn best practices to improve RAG system outputs. You’ll also explore naive, advanced, and modular RAG implementation techniques and discover best practices to overcome challenges associated with a RAG system. Duration: 4 hours. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Enhanced AI Outputs

doc: Course: Craft Precise Prompts for AI Models. Description: In this module, you’ll explore how to write prompts to generate required output from generative AI models such as large language models (LLMs). You’ll also learn how to create prompt templates with variables to generate output from LLMs. Duration: 75 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Craft Precise Prompts for AI Models

doc: Course: Building AI Solutions Using Advanced Algorithms and Open Source Frameworks. Description: This badge earner demonstrates knowledge and understanding in technical and scientific concepts related to building AI solutions with open-source frameworks (such as TensorFlow and Keras) to make model predictions using regression, classification, and clustering algorithms such as Naive Bayes, Logistic regression, K-nearest neighbors, Polynomial Linear Regression, SVM (Kernel), Decision tree, and Ensemble learning, K means and DBSCAN for anomaly detection and computer vision. Duration: 20+ hours. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Building AI Solutions Using Advanced Algorithms and Open Source Frameworks

doc: Course: Building Trustworthy AI Enterprise Solutions. Description: This badge earner has completed all the learning activities included in this online learning experience, including hands-on experience, concepts, methods, and tools related to exploring the ethical adoption of AI technologies in the enterprise. The individual has demonstrated skills and understanding of the use of AI technologies for business, including: AI industry adoption trends, AI ethical considerations, Natural Language Processing, and Virtual Agents. Duration: 10+ hours. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Building Trustworthy AI Enterprise Solutions

doc: Course: Code Generation and Optimization Using IBM Granite. Description: In this module, you’ll explore how to use IBM Granite models to address a variety of programming challenges. You’ll learn how to generate efficient, reliable, and adaptable code tailored to your project’s needs and optimize the code to improve its performance. Duration: 210 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Code Generation and Optimization Using IBM Granite

doc: Course: Data Classification and  Summarization Using IBM Granite. Description: In this module, you’ll explore how to construct, judge, and improve prompts for classifying and summarizing data. You’ll practice prompting an IBM Granite model to classify and summarize data, and discover how to adjust model parameters for enhanced output quality, model outputs, and use evaluation insights to adjust prompts and parameters. Finally, you’ll practice adjusting the model parameters to refine the output. Duration: 180-240 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Data Classification and  Summarization Using IBM Granite

doc: Course: Intelligent by Design: Build an AI Agent. Description: Are you interested in exploring how to build an AI agent? In this module, you discover how AI agents work, explore different agent types, and learn how to structure effective workflows. You’ll also evaluate performance, apply ethical design principles, and prepare your agent for deployment in real-world scenarios. Duration: 4 hours. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Intelligent by Design: Build an AI Agent

doc: Course: AI-Enabled Applications for Customer Service. Description: This course explores the role of artificial intelligence in transforming customer service operations. It covers the fundamentals of AI technologies, their application in customer service, and their impact on businesses and customer experiences. You will learn how to design and implement AI-driven customer service systems, including chatbots, sentiment analysis, personalization, and more to improve customer experience. Duration: 8 hours. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: AI-Enabled Applications for Customer Service

doc: Course: Generative AI in Action. Description: This credential earner has applied technical knowledge of principles of generative AI, prompt engineering techniques, and Python libraries. The individual has demonstrated general knowledge of the methods, applications, and ethical considerations of using GenAI models. The earner has practiced essential workplace skills and explored generative AI career pathways. Duration: 5+ hours. Category: Artificial intelligence.

meta.title: Generative AI in Action

doc: Course: Artificial Intelligence Practitioner Pathway. Description: The Artificial Intelligence Practitioner Pathway prepares you for careers in AI through the learning of AI concepts such as machine learning, deep learning, natural language processing, data analytics, AI ethics, and more. You will explore use cases and applications of AI and experience AI through simulations and labs. Whether you are a beginner exploring the field or an experienced professional seeking to expand your knowledge, this course will provide you with a solid foundation in artificial intelligence and its role in the workplace and society. Duration: 10+ hours. Category: Artificial intelligence Labs.

meta.title: Artificial Intelligence Practitioner Pathway

doc: Course: Create a chatbot with IBM watsonx Assistant. Description: In this lab, you will learn how to build a chatbot and add various actions and steps to create an interactive and intelligent assistant for your website. Duration: 30 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence Labs.

meta.title: Create a chatbot with IBM watsonx Assistant

doc: Course: Energy Demand Forecasting with IBM Granite Time Series. Description: Forecasting in time series analysis allows data scientists to identify patterns by using machine learning and then generate forecasts about the future. TinyTimeMixers (TTMs) are compact pre-trained models for Multivariate Time-Series Forecasting.  The goal of this lab is to show how you can predict future trends on historical data using the IBM Granite Time Series models. Duration: 30 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence Labs.

meta.title: Energy Demand Forecasting with IBM Granite Time Series

doc: Course: Entity Extraction from Description Related to a Book Using IBM Granite. Description: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable accuracy in the task of entity extraction. In this lab, you will learn how to extract key entities from descriptions related to books using IBM Granite-8B. Duration: 30 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence Labs.

meta.title: Entity Extraction from Description Related to a Book Using IBM Granite

doc: Course: Explore machine learning and data science in IBM watsonx. Description: Learn how to use the machine learning and data science capabilities of IBM watsonx to process and analyze data, build a binary classification model, and build a multivariate time series forecasting model. Duration: 30 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence Labs.

meta.title: Explore machine learning and data science in IBM watsonx

doc: Course: Document Summarization with IBM Granite. Description: This lab showcases the essential capabilities of IBM Granite models through bite-sized instructional notebooks. In this lab, you will apply summarization techniques using IBM Granite. Duration: 30 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence Labs.

meta.title: Document Summarization with IBM Granite

doc: Course: Prompt a Foundation Model in IBM watsonx. Description: In this lab, you’ll be introduced to the generative AI features in IBM watsonx.ai, the Prompt Lab, foundation models, prompt engineering, and workspaces, and then get hands-on experience with prompting a foundation model. Duration: 30 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence Labs.

meta.title: Prompt a Foundation Model in IBM watsonx

doc: Course: Retrieval Augmented Generation with LangChain. Description: This notebook contains instructions for performing Retrieval Augumented Generation (RAG). RAG is an architectural pattern that can be used to augment the performance of language models by recalling factual information from a knowledge base, and adding that information to the model query. Duration: 30 minutes total course time. Category: Artificial intelligence Labs.

meta.title: Retrieval Augmented Generation with LangChain

doc: Course: Build an AI-Powered Document Retrieval System with IBM Granite and Docling. Description: In this lab, you will learn how to take a set of source documents, convert them into text and then split the text into chunks, derive embedding vectors using the embedding model, and load it into the vector database. Creating this vector database will allow you to easily search across the documents, enabling you to use Retrieval-augmented generation. Duration: 30 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence Labs.

meta.title: Build an AI-Powered Document Retrieval System with IBM Granite and Docling

doc: Course: Build an AI-Powered Multimodal RAG System with IBM Granite and Docling. Description: Multimodal RAG can use multimodal LLMs (MLLM) to process information from multiple types of data to be included as part of the external knowledge base used in RAG. In this lab, you’ll learn to harness the power of advanced tools to build an AI-powered multimodal RAG pipeline. Duration: 30 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence Labs.

meta.title: Build an AI-Powered Multimodal RAG System with IBM Granite and Docling

doc: Course: Craft Responsible Prompts. Description: Responsible Prompting is an LLM-agnostic tool that aims at dynamically supporting users in crafting prompts that reflect responsible intentions and help avoid undesired or negative outputs. This lab covers crafting prompts using the IBM Granite Embedding Models in the Responsible Prompting API. Duration: 30 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence Labs.

meta.title: Craft Responsible Prompts

doc: Course: Implement RAG Using LlamaIndex Orchestration Framework. Description: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is a cutting-edge approach that combines the strengths of information retrieval and generative AI models. By integrating external knowledge sources, RAG enables AI systems to generate more accurate and contextually relevant responses. This lab covers implementing a simple Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture using the LlamaIndex orchestration layer and a knowledge base stored in ChromaDB. Duration: 30 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence Labs.

meta.title: Implement RAG Using LlamaIndex Orchestration Framework

doc: Course: Implement a ReAct Design Pattern with IBM Granite. Description: In this lab, you will learn about the ReAct design pattern and its essentials, you will implement ReAct from the ground up and be introduced to techniques to level up ReAct for more complex use cases and production ready settings. Duration: 30 minutes. Category: Artificial intelligence Labs.

meta.title: Implement a ReAct Design Pattern with IBM Granite

doc: Course: Use Inference Scaling to Improve Multimodal RAG. Description: Inference scaling in AI enhances model performance by optimizing the use of computational resources during this inference phase. In this lab, you’ll learn to harness the power of inference scaling to improve the multimodal RAG pipeline created in a previous lab. Duration: 30 minutes. Category: Cybersecurity.

meta.title: Use Inference Scaling to Improve Multimodal RAG

doc: Course: Getting Started with Cybersecurity. Description: This credential earner demonstrates a foundational understanding of cybersecurity key elements and the threats it addresses, including concepts of data privacy, and how to evaluate data security using the CIA triad model. The earner is aware of the job outlook in cybersecurity and is familiar with the skills required for success in various roles. The individual understands how a cybersecurity tool works. Duration: 3 hours. Category: Cybersecurity.

meta.title: Getting Started with Cybersecurity

doc: Course: Cybersecurity Fundamentals. Description: This badge earner demonstrates a foundational understanding of cybersecurity concepts, objectives, and practices. This includes cyber threat groups, types of attacks, social engineering, case studies, overall cyber security risk management strategies, cryptography, and common approaches that organizations take to prevent, detect, and respond to cyber attacks. This also includes an awareness of the job market. Badge earners can use this knowledge to pursue further education for a variety of roles in cybersecurity. Duration: 7.5 hours. Category: Cybersecurity.

meta.title: Cybersecurity Fundamentals

doc: Course: Level Up Cybersecurity with Generative AI. Description: In this module, you’ll explore features of generative AI used in cybersecurity. You’ll discover how gen AI helps with cybersecurity incidents by enabling faster threat detection, better decision making, and automating time-consuming tasks. You’ll also learn how gen AI handles specific cybersecurity problems in the healthcare, finance, and public sectors. Duration: 1 hour 30 minutes. Category: Cybersecurity.

meta.title: Level Up Cybersecurity with Generative AI

doc: Course: Enterprise Security in Practice. Description: This badge earner has completed all the learning activities included in this online learning experience, including hands-on experience, concepts, methods and tools related to the enterprise security domain. The individual has demonstrated skills and understanding in the approaches to elevate an organization’s overall security posture, by adopting practices, methods and tools that increase enterprise cyber resilience. Duration: 10+ hours. Category: Cybersecurity.

meta.title: Enterprise Security in Practice

doc: Course: Getting Started with Threat Intelligence and Hunting. Description: This badge earner has completed all the learning activities included in this online learning experience, including hands-on experience, concepts, methods and tools related to the threat intelligence and hunting domain. The individual has demonstrated domain knowledge and understanding in adopting practices, methods and tools that relates to the activities performed in cyber threat hunting. Duration: 5 hours. Category: Cybersecurity.

meta.title: Getting Started with Threat Intelligence and Hunting

doc: Course: Security Operations Center in Practice. Description: This badge earner has completed all the learning activities included in this online learning experience, including hands-on experience, concepts, methods and tools related to the Security Operations Center’s domain. The individual has developed skills around techniques, technologies, roles and scenarios needed to establish the foundations of a Security Operations Center (SOC) within an organization. Duration: 20+ hours. Category: Cybersecurity.

meta.title: Security Operations Center in Practice

doc: Course: Cybersecurity Fluency Pathway. Description: As technology advances, ensuring the security of digital assets and protecting the online presence of individuals and organizations have emerged as critical priorities. Every industry now finds itself in need of skilled cybersecurity professionals. Whether you are a beginner exploring the field or an experienced professional seeking to expand your knowledge, this course will provide you with a solid foundation in cybersecurity. You will delve into key concepts, terminology, and essential principles, and engage in activities with tools, frameworks, and scenarios to help you apply and practice what you’ve learned. Duration: 54 hours. Category: Data science.

meta.title: Cybersecurity Fluency Pathway

doc: Course: Getting Started with Data. Description: This credential earner demonstrates a foundational understanding of data concepts, such as types of data, big data, the data analytics process, data visualization, and the data science landscape. The individual has worked with data analytics tool to import, analyze and visualize data. Duration: 3 hours. Category: Data science.

meta.title: Getting Started with Data

doc: Course: Supercharge Your Data Analytics with Generative AI. Description: In this module, you’ll gain a practical understanding of how generative AI is reshaping data analytics. You’ll learn to classify core generative AI capabilities such as automation and synthetic data generation. You’ll also explore how these tools apply at different stages of the data analytics process, from data preparation to insight delivery. Finally, you’ll discover the key risks that come with these technologies and what responsible AI use looks like in real-world analytics settings. Duration: 75 minutes. Category: Data science.

meta.title: Supercharge Your Data Analytics with Generative AI

doc: Course: Data Fundamentals. Description: This credential earner demonstrates knowledge of data analytics concepts, methodologies and applications of data science, and the tools and programming languages used in the data ecosystem. The individual has a conceptual understanding of how to clean, refine, and visualize data using IBM Watson Studio. The earner is aware of the job outlook in data and is familiar with the skills required for success in various roles in the domain. Duration: 7 hours. Category: Data science.

meta.title: Data Fundamentals

doc: Course: Enterprise Data Science in Practice. Description: This badge earner has completed all the learning activities included in this online learning experience including hands-on labs, concepts, methods, and tools related to the data science methodology. They demonstrate skills and understanding of the Data Science methodology by engaging in real-wold scenarios and role-playing the process/tools used by a data science team to address real-world enterprise challenges in the insurance industry leveraging cutting-edge fraud analytics approaches and technologies. Duration: 10+ hours. Category: Data science.

meta.title: Enterprise Data Science in Practice

doc: Course: Machine Learning for Data Science Projects. Description: This earner completed all learning activities included in this online learning experience related to advanced topics core to the data science profession including: Data Modeling techniques; Machine Learning; Deep Learning algorithms; Data Science Automation; demonstration of advanced skills application in the field of Data Science by role-playing critical roles in a data science team using latest AI tools for analytics/automation to address real problems. Duration: 20+ hours. Category: Data science.

meta.title: Machine Learning for Data Science Projects

doc: Course: Data Analytics Fluency Pathway. Description: In today’s data-driven world, being able to extract meaningful insights from data is an invaluable skill. Every industry is now finding itself in need of skilled data analytics professionals. Whether you are a beginner exploring the field or an experienced professional seeking to expand your knowledge, this course will provide you with a solid foundation in using data to help drive informed decision making. You will explore real-world scenarios, navigate data sets, and craft compelling visual narratives to help you apply and practice what you’re learning. You will delve into key concepts, terminology, and essential principles that will make you fluent in this crucial discipline. Duration: 52 hours. Category: Cloud.

meta.title: Data Analytics Fluency Pathway

doc: Course: Cloud Computing Fundamentals. Description: This credential earner demonstrates knowledge of cloud computing, including cloud services, deployment models, virtualization, orchestration, and cloud security. The individual is aware of cloud benefits for users and businesses. The individual has a conceptual understanding of how to create a container, deploy a web app to the cloud, and analyze security in a simulated environment. The earner is aware of the job outlook in cloud computing and the skills required for success in various roles. Duration: 10+ hours. Category: Cloud.

meta.title: Cloud Computing Fundamentals

doc: Course: Journey to Cloud: Envisioning Your Solution. Description: This credential earner demonstrates knowledge and understanding of the digital transformation drivers made possible by cloud technologies and services. This includes: how cloud works, its capabilities, types, and delivery models (IaaS, SaaS, and PaaS); digital transformation strategies such as Agile practices, the IBM Garage Method, and Enterprise Design Thinking to help organizations get started on their transformation journey; and deploying a test pilot cloud application using IBM Code Engine. Duration: 5 hours. Category: Cloud.

meta.title: Journey to Cloud: Envisioning Your Solution

doc: Course: Journey to Cloud: Orchestrating Your Solution. Description: This credential earner demonstrates knowledge and understanding in the intricate relationship between the enterprises embracing the journey to Cloud adoption and the cloud service providers. The credential earner acquired advanced skills on cloud computing adoption concepts such as compliance, data management, data protection, cloud security, service management and operations, ITIL, incident, problem and change management, site reliability engineering, AIOps, and governance. Duration: 20+ hours. Category: Cloud.

meta.title: Journey to Cloud: Orchestrating Your Solution

doc: Course: Journey to Cloud: Transforming Your Culture. Description: This credential earner demonstrates knowledge and understanding in cloud adoption concepts such as cloud culture, modern cloud practices, and the corresponding roles, with a focus on cloud development practices such as DevOps & Agile. The credential earner explores technical concepts such as cloud-native architecture, microservices, containerization, and APIs and understands what organizational changes may be required when an organization decides to migrate its workloads to the cloud. Duration: 10+ hours. Category: Not finding what you’re looking for?.

meta.category: Not finding what you’re looking for?
meta.title: Journey to Cloud: Transforming Your Culture
